lead_profile_text: Developer with newly completed block of 14 x 1-bed flats in Nottingham Sherwood. Deciding between: sell block, LTL all, or STL some/all. Per flat: gross ~£23,000/yr (£103.98/night, 62% occ), net ~£12,000/yr before utilities. Utilities ~£300/mo per flat leaves net ~£700/mo. LTL gross £800-850/mo per flat. STL advantage minimal after utilities. Developer needs >30% STL uplift to justify complexity — current 1-bed Nottingham projections do not meet this threshold. Recommended: mixed strategy (some LTL for stability, some STL for upside). Short 11-minute meeting.
emotional_profile: Analytical developer mindset. Sets a clear 30% uplift threshold before accepting STL complexity. Pragmatic about mixed strategies. Not emotionally invested in STL — purely financial decision. Language: '30% higher income to justify the extra complexity', 'six or seven long-term, a few short-term'.

top_objections.text: STL advantage over LTL is minimal for 1-bed Nottingham after utilities — doesn't meet 30% uplift threshold
top_objections.zac_response: Mixed strategy recommended — some LTL for stability, some STL for upside; can pilot STL on a subset before committing full portfolio

top_objections.text: Added complexity of managing STL across 14 units needs to be justified by significant income uplift
top_objections.zac_response: Framed as selective STL — not all 14 units need to switch; start with 3-4 as a pilot

top_questions.text: What is the realistic net income per flat from STL vs LTL?
top_questions.answer: STL gross ~£23,000/yr, net ~£12,000/yr before utilities; LTL ~£9,600/yr — marginal uplift after £300/mo utilities per unit

top_questions.text: What strategy is recommended for a developer portfolio of 14 x 1-bed flats?
top_questions.answer: Mixed strategy — some LTL for stable income, some STL for upside; needs >30% uplift to justify full STL conversion

top_questions.text: Can we pilot STL on a subset of units first?
top_questions.answer: Yes — recommended approach for large portfolios to test STL performance before full commitment

what_moved_them: Mixed strategy concept resonated — doesn't have to be all-or-nothing
Zac was transparent that 1-bed Nottingham STL advantage is marginal — built trust through honesty
what_fell_flat: STL income projections for 1-bed Nottingham don't meet Alan's own 30% uplift threshold
Meeting was very short (11 minutes) — insufficient depth of engagement
No strong conversion momentum developed in a short session

conversion_gaps.gap: STL financials for 1-bed Nottingham don't meet developer's 30% uplift threshold
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Identify which units in the block have best STL potential (corner units, higher floors, better specification) — target those for pilot; avoid trying to convert marginal units

conversion_gaps.gap: Short 11-minute meeting — insufficient to build relationship or full conviction
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Book a longer follow-up session specifically on portfolio strategy; share developer client case studies who ran mixed LTL/STL portfolios

zac_income_intro: Per flat: gross ~£23,000/yr at £103.98/night and 62% occupancy — net ~£12,000/yr before utilities of ~£300/mo
zac_transition_phrases: 30% higher income to justify the extra complexity
six or seven long-term, a few short-term
